summary: Admin Box Audit — iteration 260. Tested Product Box archive/restore, Service Box archive/restore/edit, Soul Box generate-image routing, admin services filtering, and frontend UI. 28/28 backend tests pass. All core features work correctly. One pre-existing data corruption bug found causing React duplicate key warnings.

minor.issue: products_master collection has TWO documents with id='bp-akita-designer-bandana-969f46' (one with old name 'Akita Designer Bandana', one with new name 'Akita · Designer Bandana · Birthday Edition'). Both are returned in the API response causing React duplicate key warning. Root cause: MASTER SYNC or seeding created duplicate entries. Code dedup only prevents breed_products-vs-products_master duplicates, NOT within-products_master duplicates.

Supplemental breed_products query uses breed_query={} with NO visibility.status filter. This means archived breed_products can appear in active list and active breed_products can appear in archived list. Affects accuracy of supplemental results only.

ui_bugs.issue: React console error: 'Encountered two children with the same key bp-akita-designer-bandana-969f46'. Caused by data corruption in products_master (two documents with same id). Fix: deduplicate products_master or add unique index on id field.

backend.TestProductBoxArchiveRestore: 7/7 PASS — archive via DELETE, verify not in active list, verify in archived, restore via PATCH, verify active after restore
backend.TestServiceBoxArchiveRestore: 7/7 PASS — create test service, archive via DELETE /api/service-box/services/{id}, not in admin list, in archived endpoint, restore, back in admin list
backend.TestServiceBoxEdit: 1/1 PASS — PUT /api/admin/services/{id} saves name+description, verified via GET
backend.TestAdminServicesFiltering: 1/1 PASS — GET /api/admin/services returns zero archived services
backend.TestAdminDeleteServiceSoftArchives: 3/3 PASS — DELETE /api/admin/services/{id} soft-archives, service still exists with approval_status=archived
backend.TestSoulBoxBreedProductArchiveRestore: 4/4 PASS — breed product archive/restore via product-box endpoints, restored breed product is active
backend.TestSoulBoxGenerateImageRouting: 3/3 PASS — /api/admin/generate-image requires prompt (400), breed-products generate-image endpoint exists, service-box generate-image endpoint exists
backend.TestServiceBoxArchivedEndpoint: 2/2 PASS — GET /api/service-box/services/archived returns 200 with services, all have approval_status=archived

frontend.ServiceBox_ShowArchivedToggle: PASS — toggle button '📦 Show Archived' visible, changes to '← Back to Active'
frontend.ServiceBox_RestoreButtons: PASS — restore buttons appear only in archived view, edit/delete buttons hidden
frontend.ServiceBox_RestoreFlow: PASS — clicking restore shows toast 'TEST AdminDelete 449260 restored', service removed from archived list
frontend.SoulBox_ArchiveRestoreButtons: PASS — Archive buttons (50) and Restore buttons (50) visible in archived view
frontend.SoulBox_StatusFilter: PASS — 'Archived only' option in status dropdown
frontend.SoulBox_ProductBoxEditor: PASS — editor opens with multi-tab interface, Save Product button visible
frontend.ProductBox_ArchiveButton: PASS — delete/archive buttons visible on products

action_items: FIX DATA: Remove duplicate documents from products_master collection — there are two documents with id='bp-akita-designer-bandana-969f46' (old: 'Akita Designer Bandana', new: 'Akita · Designer Bandana · Birthday Edition'). Prefer keeping the new-format one. Add MASTER SYNC step to deduplicate products_master by id before inserting.

FIX CODE (minor): In get_all_products (unified_product_box.py line 790), the breed_query={} doesn't apply visibility.status filter. When status=archived is requested, breed_products appear without status filter. Fix: copy the visibility.status condition from main query into breed_query.

FIX CODE (robustness): The dedup in get_all_products uses O(n) linear scan (any() loop). Replace with a set for O(1) lookups: seen_ids = {mp.get('id') for mp in all_products}; if pid not in seen_ids: seen_ids.add(pid); all_products.append(p)
critical_code_review_comments: DATA CORRUPTION: products_master has two docs with same id='bp-akita-designer-bandana-969f46'. Add unique index on 'id' field in both products_master and breed_products to prevent future duplicates.
BUG: breed_products supplement in get_all_products ignores visibility.status filter (breed_query={}), causing mixed active/archived results in supplemental products.
DEDUP: Using any() O(n) scan instead of set lookup — for large collections this is slow. Use seen_ids set.

success_rate.frontend: 90% (all features work; 1 React duplicate key warning from data corruption)

seed_data_creation: Test services created with IDs TEST-AR-* and TEST-AD-* (all cleaned up/archived after tests)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All admin box audit features pass. Pre-existing data corruption: products_master has duplicate id='bp-akita-designer-bandana-969f46' causing React duplicate key warning. ServiceBox 'Show Archived' toggle + restore confirmed working. Product Box archive/restore confirmed working. Admin DELETE services confirmed soft-archives. Soul Box generate-image uses correct entity_type/entity_id. Breed product archive/restore via product-box endpoints works correctly. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_admin_box_audit_260.py

duplicate_key_react_error.symptom: React console error: 'Encountered two children with same key bp-akita-designer-bandana-969f46'
duplicate_key_react_error.root_cause: products_master MongoDB collection has TWO documents with id='bp-akita-designer-bandana-969f46' — one with old naming format 'Akita Designer Bandana' and one with new format 'Akita · Designer Bandana · Birthday Edition'. Both have soul_made=True and visibility.status=active. The API returns both, causing React to render two items with the same key.
duplicate_key_react_error.reproduction: GET /api/product-box/products?soul_made=true&limit=50 returns two items with id='bp-akita-designer-bandana-969f46'
duplicate_key_react_error.mitigation: 1) MongoDB: db.products_master.deleteOne({id:'bp-akita-designer-bandana-969f46', name:'Akita Designer Bandana'}) to remove the old duplicate. 2) Code: Add unique index on id field. 3) MASTER SYNC: Add dedup step to remove products_master duplicates.
duplicate_key_react_error.introduced_by: Pre-existing data corruption — likely from two seed operations for same breed+product_type with different naming conventions generating the same hash-based ID
